patches.title: Temtem: Pioneers has been revealed!
patches.author: Tsukki
patches.content:
Hello, Tamers!
The time has finally come for us to show you [b]Temtem: Pioneers[/b], our [b]newest project and the next step in the Temtem IP[/b]. We wanted to onboard everyone by sharing more about what you’ll discover and encounter in the world of Temtem: Pioneers, but first: take a look at our amazing trailer!
[previewyoutube=hbWkpX49FnU;full][/previewyoutube]
[h2]Welcome to a new frontier![/h2]
Your character has suffered an unexpected accident while aboard the Narwhal, and it has [b]landed them right in the Downbelow[/b]! Create your character and prepare to face the perils of this unexplored land. The good news? You won’t be alone! [b]The world of Pioneers is teeming with Temtem[/b], some [b]familiar and some new Temtem[/b] to discover in the Downbelow to battle and befriend. Find your first Temtem partner to tackle this world together, and expand your Temtem collection as you progress.
[h2]Survive and thrive[/h2]
Temtem: Pioneers is a [b]survival and crafting game[/b], so your biggest priority is to find a way to persevere in an environment both new and strange, all alone, and devoid of all the technology the Airborne Archipelago up there has developed. Craft tools, gather resources, and develop gears and trinkets to make your way across the unknown.
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/46072883/1c6b9b2b1750f2355a9bbb372c772f57e9e30b3a.jpg[/img]
[h2]A home away from home[/h2]
Next step on your list: a [b]place to sleep and rest[/b] for both you and your Temtem. Build a home with the [b]materials you’ve gathered[/b], and take shelter. For those (like me) who aren’t great at building, fear not: a [b]unique new building system will help[/b] you make the home of your dreams a reality in-game too!
[h2]A stunning world to explore[/h2]
The Downbelow is vast, and it’s all open to you! Explore a myriad of [b]unique biomes[/b], from peaceful meadows to ancient ruins, scorching volcanoes and creepy swamps. The world you’ll find yourself in is[b] wild, untamed and beautiful[/b], but also perilous. [b]Fearsome bosses[/b] await your arrival, protecting new biomes and further progress.
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/46072883/305e9d6297c3d5609c2afd902a7e17873356ba5d.jpg[/img]
That's all we can reveal for now! We appreciate all the love and support you have always shown us and the Temtem IP, and we’re super eager to invite you on this new adventure! As a new Kickstarter campaign begins today, we'd love to have your love and support once more: [url=https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/crema/temtem-project-downbelow/?utm_source=crosspromo&utm_campaign=kscampaign&utm_medium=temtem]back Temtem: Pîoneers on Kickstarter![/url]
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/46072883/8fc9bcc2caa4dbcf66205b26bfbea8880a490395.jpg[/img]
If you're not into Kickstarter backing, you can also wishlisth the game on [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/2961930/Temtem_Pioneers/?utm_source=crosspromo&utm_campaign=fullrelease&utm_medium=temtem]Steam[/url] or in the [url=https://store.epicgames.com/p/temtem-pioneers-1e978b/?utm_source=post&utm_campaign=kscampaign&utm_medium=steam]Epic Games Store[/url] and support us without spending a penny!
And if you're filled with excitement and want to talk about it, come meet other Tamers and theorycraft together in our official [url=https://discord.gg/XtzYSDUVBW]Temtem Discord server.[/url]
Answer the call of the Downbelow, and Temtem up!

patches.title: Temtem: Swarm has reached 1.0!
patches.author: Tsukki
patches.content:
[h2]Hello, Tamers! [/h2]
[b]Temtem: Swarm,[/b] the bullet-heave spin off in the Temtem IP [b]has just reached patch 1.0[/b]!
After our year and a half Early Access adventure, during which community feedback has deeply marked development, we're ready to raise the curtain on the [b]complete version of Temtem: Swarm[/b]!
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/56e7e337c843708c5a093f2e5b6362d7793481a9.jpg[/img]
The 1.0 update allows you to complete everything that was previously inaccessible, and gives you access to [b]two new Tems, a new Arena, Lumas, Umbras, the Tempedia, Prestige System[/b], and much more!
In case you're interested, you can read a complete rundown of new features, improvements, balance, bug fixes and more [url=https://store.steampowered.com/news/app/2510960/view/503980253269459765?l=english]on the full patch notes!
[/url]
Such a special occasion deserves celebration, and so we've [b]teamed up[/b] with two of the best survivors out there for sweet, discounted bundles! Buying any of these bundles will add a pretty [b]10% off[/b] for both games.
[b]Rock, Stone, and Swarm![/b]
Who knew dwarves and Temtem could go so well together? Experience the Deep Rock universe and the Temtem universe from a different perspective in these bullet-heaven survivors full of excitement and fast-paced action.
[dynamiclink href="https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/54276/Deep_Rock_Galactic_Survivors__Temtem_Swarm/?utm_source=post&utm_campaign=fullrelease&utm_medium=steam"][/dynamiclink]
[b]PotaTem[/b]
Get tactical and overwhelm your enemies in either Brotato or Temtem: Swarm! Give in to the abundant characters, the myriad of builds, and the bullets and Techniques flying everywhere. You'll find yourself unable to play just one run!
[dynamiclink href="https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/62302/Brotato__Temtem_Swarm/?utm_source=post&utm_campaign=fullrelease&utm_medium=steam"][/dynamiclink]
And if you're interested in getting Temtem: Swarm alone, we also have a nice discount as part of the Daily Deal on Steam. It'll last from now until the 16th! Get a 30% off to celebrate Swarm's 1.0 launch, and enjoy your bullet-heaven adventure to the fullest.
[dynamiclink href="https://store.steampowered.com/app/2510960/Temtem_Swarm/?utm_source=post&utm_campaign=fullrelease&utm_medium=steam"][/dynamiclink]See you in the Evershifting Tower and...
[h1]Happy launch day, Tamers! [/h1]

patches.title: Project Downbelow will be at the Triple-i Initiative on April 9th!
patches.author: Tsukki
patches.content:
The Triple-i Initiative is coming back on the 9th of April at 9am PT/6pm CET, and we’re very happy to announce that Project Downbelow will be there!
The show is only back-to-back trailers of games from other studios and us. A whopping 45 minutes - of world premieres, gameplay reveals, launch dates, demo drops and more.
There's no hosts, ads or sponsors – just games!
It'll be streamed on [url=https://www.youtube.com/@iii_initiative]Youtube[/url], [url=https://www.twitch.tv/iii_initiative]Twitch[/url] and right here on Steam!
You can watch the announcement trailer below, or check out https://iii-initiative.com/ for more information.
[previewyoutube=3AsSAM2EWKw;full][/previewyoutube]
That’s all we can say for now, but we hope you'll tune in! It’s gonna be fun and we’re mega excited to show you more of what we’ve been cooking! For the fans of the Temtem universe, you are in for a ride!
Until then, have fun and Temtem up!

patches.title: Patch 1.8.3 lands on Temtem!
patches.author: Tsukki
patches.content:
[h1]Important notice
[/h1]
[list]
[*]We've adjusted the way Spectator mode and the Skip Animations setting worked:
[list]
[*]An [b]spectator can't have Skip Animations ON[/b] if [b]none of the players[/b] [b]has Skip animations ON[/b].[/*]
[*]If [b]one of the players has the setting turned OFF[/b] (so they watch the animations), the spectator [b]can still skip the animations[/b], since the match will wait for the player who sees animations to complete their turn.[/*]
[*][b]If both players[/b] have[b] Skip Animations ON,[/b] the [b]Spectator[/b] can have the [b]animations skipped [/b]as well.[/*]
[*]While you're spectating a match, the option to Skip Animations will be blocked in your settings, and it will be adjusted[/*]
[*]Once you quit spectating the match, the setting will go back to the way you had previously set it.[/*]
[*]The reason for this change was the many issues the interaction between these two features were causing. If an spectator was watching animations but the players weren't, turn actions would get overlapped, VFX would play all at once, and could even cause black screens.[/*][/list][/list]
[list]
[*]The Ranked Season will [b]end on March 3rd insted of on April 7th[/b].
[list]
[*]A bug caused this Ranked Season to have an extra month. It has since been corrected.[/*]
[*]From this patch onwards, all Ranked Seasons will [b]last for 2 months[/b], as originally intended.[/*][/list] [/list]
[list]
[*]The code from the [b]February Newsletter[/b] is now [b]working correctly[/b], sorry for the inconveniences![/*]
[/list]
[h1]Balance[/h1]
PvP
[b]Temtem[/b]
[i]We feel like we've gotten to a point where all Tems are equally balanced and have balanced pick&ban rates, so the balance section is the shortest we've ever written. We're always on the case, so if your feedback or the data we gather prove otherwise, we'll look into it.[/i]
[list]
[*][b]#063[/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Scaravolt[/b]
[list]
[*]Magma Cannon has been added back to its Tech Pool.[/*]
[/list]
[/*]
[/list]
[h1]Bug Fixes[/h1]
[b]All platforms[/b]
[list]
[*]Fixed getting hard-locked out of the game if a player loses connection right as they enter a GritArena match.[/*]
[*]Fixed a black screen after leaving spectator mode during a turn if we had the Skip Animations setting turned off.[/*]
[*]Fixed a black screen caused by the animation of aa rival Galios fainting failing to play after it had fainted via Doom by having its Trait deactivated.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed getting softlocked by the UI after leaving a PvP match or a Replay.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed getting an invisible NPC after doing a couple of GritArena runs. Interacting with these NPCs causes a softlock.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed losing our Gear if we handed an NPC a Gear-wearing Temtem directly from our TemDeck.[/*]
[*]Fixed that the cinematic with the Guards blocking access to Quetzal wasn't playing correctly for Co-op players, forcing them to break the Co-op.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed a visual bug where our would Temtem appear at 1 HP after winning a tie-breaker in a PvE match.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed having our Temtem appear at 0 HP on the Evershifting Tower, as well as in some situations like the Melee Dojo or the Arbury Tournament.[/*]
[*]Fixed encountering Tems over level 100 in the Temtem Nodes of the Mythical lairs, when the limit is 100.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed not seeing the message that a radar has broken, on certain places and only if the player had the Skip Animations settings turned on.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed an incorrect interaction between Viral Combustion and Voodoo when using a Tech that applied two Status Conditions.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed 2 Temtem on Sophia's Dojo Rematch team having the same Gear.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed seeing a permanent notification on the Tamer Pass to claim an item if we had gotten said item through any other medium, such as a Newsletter gift.[/*]
[*]👥Fixed seeing the visual after effects of Battle Outros when finishing a match or a Replay.[/*]
[/list]
[b]Xbox[/b]
[list]
[*]Fixed that the sound caused by the mechanism in Braeside Castle was playing louder than in other platforms.[/*]
[/list]
[u]Fixes, features, and improvements marked with the 👥 icon are originally suggested, inspired by, or reported by our community in our [url=http://discord.gg/Temtem]Discord server [/url]and [url=https://www.reddit.com/r/PlayTemtem/]subreddit[/url].[/u]

patches.title: The road well-traveled: Patch 1.8 and all its meaning
patches.author: Tsukki
patches.content:
Hey there, Tamers! We just reached 2.000.000 accounts on Temtem, which is an impossibly big number for us to fathom! As we achieve this milestone, we think it's a great time to reflect on patch 1.8, which was the last big planned patch for Temtem and such an important milestone for us to reach, as well.
The focus on patch 1.8 was to make Temtem more enjoyable in a more casual way, putting aside our long-held fears that having things become too easy would destroy or make impossible a proper, stable player economy. Screw that!
We made adjustments to most gameplay areas so goals were more achievable and accessible, and most things can now be obtained in less time. We did our best to make sure the game is enjoyable, fun and kind to all the players who continue enjoying Temtem on a daily basis, and any new players who might make their way into the Archipelago.
There was hardly any area of gameplay untouched in the patch, so we recommend you give [url=https://crema.gg/temtem/patch-1-8/]the patch notes[/url] a read if you didn't back then, but today I'll do my best to break down the most important changes we made to Temtem.
Join me in this stroll through the many changes and improvements in the 1.8 update!
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/82f224951e5f8dc99e6d8ca68122d1177dae52ef.jpg[/img]
[h1]We have Umbras at home (and we really do)[/h1]
In this new Temtem era, the dream of having Luma and Umbra Tems has already become a reality for many more players! To start off easy, we [b]improved the base Luma and Umbra rates to 1/2000 and 1/20000[/b], respectively. That said, we still wanted to keep the flow of new Lumas/Umbras comprehensible and sensible, because they are still special! To strike a good balance, we [b]made some adjustments to Radars and the Saipark[/b], which were the main source of Lumas and Umbras for most players.
Luma Radars are now [b]cheaper[/b], which is good on its own, and we also added a new [b]Umbra Radar Box[/b] that can provide any random Umbra Radar from the pool, but [b]Radars[/b] (of both types) [b]now break once you capture a Luma Temtem[/b] (if it's a Luma Radar) [b]or Umbra Temtem[/b] (if it's an Umbra Radar).
Saipark got a new, similar limitation on the amount of Lumas you can catch [b]per week on it: one Luma Temtem[/b]. To offset this limitation, [b]all weeks have been and will be be Luma weeks[/b] in the new Saipark, and you don't need to pay any fee to enter it!
Since the one Luma per week condition can be confusing, let us go deeper into it: [b]once you catch ANY Luma Temtem in Saipark, the park will stop spawning Luma Tems[/b]. You can catch any regular Tems (non-Luma) or Umbra Tems in the Saipark without any risk to your weekly Luma catch. Alternatively, [b]you can run from or kill any Luma Temtem[/b], and that won’t use your catch of the week.[b]Only actually catching one Luma Tem counts as your catch of the week[/b]. Please note that the game doesn't differentiate between the weekly featured Luma and any other Luma, so catching a Luma Tateru that was just chilling in its Saipark house WILL count towards your weekly catch and lock you out of finding Lumas of the weekly featured Tems.
With that cleared up, did you know that [b]you can use any type of TemCard inside the Saipark[/b] since then? You can still buy SaiCards, of course, and they have a [b]x2 capture chance in the park[/b], plus they don't expire at the end of the week.
Our goal with this was removing as many as possible of the pain points of the Saipark, and making the experience a lot easier and more gratifying, which, as you will see, was the general trend of this patch. Like for Luma & Umbra and radars, we had to put down some limitations so it didn’t get out of control, even more so in Saipark’s case because of our ongoing battle with bots. It was made easier to find a Luma in Saipark, and it’s now free to enter, so most players have been rewarded with a sparkly new Tem.
We also made SaiCards a choice instead of an obligation, while allowing the most aesthetic-oriented players to keep that Saipark drip!
To sum it up: with these changes, while most players can find a Luma and/or an Umbra with relative ease, it became harder to hoard and accumulate huge numbers of them. We believed this was a good point of balance in making Luma/Umbra Tems both more achievable and still special!
Don't just take our word for it though, let the numbers do the talking. Since launching Patch1.8, we've seen a [b]304% increase in Luma captures[/b]!
That means [b]you're now 4 times more likely to find a Luma[/b]!
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/daa88e3477c39ae1c96da8ffdf1adb7d886de863.jpg[/img]
[h1]An improved Story campaign[/h1]
New players discover Temtem all the time, and we knew [b]the campaign could be a tad overwhelming at first[/b], and become a bit tedious later.
Trying to stablish a balanced economy began by looking at the early stages of play, but since we've thrown caution to the wind with the economy concerns, we focused on [b]making the economy kinder and more rewarding[/b]. An issue often mentioned was the need to backtrack to heal your Tems and thus be able to win the many NPC and wild Temtem combats you face, as the funds were originally very limited. To this end, we [b]improved the amount of Pansuns[/b] defeating an NPC Tamer grants: every NPC Tamer now provides 40 more Pansuns each.
Does it seem like a small number? It's actually the [b]difference between being able to buy a Balm or not[/b], so you won't have to go back to heal to a Temporium. In the grand scheme of things, this meant [b]27.400 more Pansuns during a completed game campaign[/b], battling all 685 NPC Tamers.
To pair with this, we created a new item, a sort of little cousin to the Temessence Phial:[b] the Temessence Ampoule[/b]. This item, which [b]heals all of your team for half of their HP[/b], is a [b]consumable[/b] that you can purchase, but most importantly, that you can find during your journey in boxes strewn across the Archipelago. This improved the feeling of having to backtrack to heal, and allowed players to keep a more straightforward strategy while they face the story campaign!
Something else that definitely helped eliminate backtracking was [b]allowing players to choose from their Temdeck when trading with an NPC[/b]. This way players no longer have to go back to a Temporium to look for the Tem they're going to part ways with.
The leveling and evolving system in Temtem took new players a bit of getting used to. To help make this easier, we [b]added the level at which a Tem would evolve[/b] to their details menu, including if it was ready to evolve in case you had previously denied the evolution. This was another frequent request and solved the issue of not knowing when your Temtem was going to evolve unless you noted down the exact level at which you caught it. Also, [b]eggs hatch faster[/b] in 1.8 than ever before!
Another subject we touched on a bit in this patch was [b]stickers[/b]! Since we wanted to make the game more enjoyable as a solo experience, we increased the amount of different stickers each person can find. We also included Stickers as a reward for Postal Service quests!
[b]Completing the album by oneself is now possible[/b], although it's going to take a lot of time! We still recommend trading with other players when possible.
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/44d6176e59112037f73bf4a48b50b313f3113aa3.jpg[/img]
[h1]What about the endgame?[/h1]
Of course, there's no alpha without omega, so we also poured tons of love into the world after the campaign: [b]the endgame[/b]. We usually consider endgame activities those that are either unlocked or only accessible after completing the campaign, plus repeatable content that provides more advanced currency or items for goals beyond finishing the campaign. This includes Tamer's Paradise, of course, but also Lairs, Housing, PvP, Luma & Umbra Hunting, etc. We made updates to most of these areas to ensure the endgame stage can be squeezed to its last drop, and every activity from Tamer's Paradise was improved upon, either to make it easier, more rewarding, or both. Let's get into it:
[b]Tamer's Paradise[/b]
First, we unified the behavior of the [b]flame icon to start after level 100 for all activities[/b]. This made the entire experience less confusing! Another change that affected all the Tamer's Paradise activities was a review and update of the TV spreads and Technique Pools of all the Tems involved, which was important so the experience was as enjoyable as possible.
Then, getting into the activities, [b]TemSafari [/b]got a reroll option for the banned types! It can be used endlessly, as long as one possesses the Pansuns for it. The leveling up curve was also softened so players could get further in the activity.
As for [b]DraftArena[/b], we softened enemy level a little, but also added a complete battle log, like the one you can see playing ranked matches! This battle log will be available even before you see the rival teams, so you can prepare well in advance.
Thinking of [b]GritArena[/b], it also got a new reroll option for the banned Tems! It works the same way as the one on TemSafari, and it'll be super helpful when your strategy requires specific Tems.
The cool [b]Evershifting Tower[/b] started letting you run away from one NPC per run, making things way easier in times of need! But even more, it has allowed you to always run away from wild Tems, and we added a complete battle log to it, which made the activity much more accessible for many.
Last but not least, the very popular [b]DigiLairs[/b]! The Tems you receive were given access to their entire Tech pools by raising their levels, and the target Tems now have better chances of being Luma or bringing the right Egg Move. We also lowered the price of the Token to 5k Pansuns, so players could repeat DigiLairs more often and have a lot more chances at Luma Tems.
And! [b]Anahir was added to the selectable characters in DigiLairs[/b], responding to a request from players to have a steady and trusted way to keep Anahir flowing into the game.[b]Mythical Lairs[/b] also had their Tems pushed to higher levels to give them access to their entire Tech pools.
To top this all off, [b]Housing[/b] fans can now have everything in the comfort of their own home! A [b]TemDeck[/b], to move Tems and edit your team; a [b]Vault[/b], with direct access to those precious rewards you get from codes or the Challenge modes; a [b]Healing Machine[/b] to heal your Temtem after arduous battle, and a totally new [b]Trading Station[/b] so you can trade, buy and sell Tems from your room!
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/7f34ffe07f6a1fa2bcf7b1a0d4584cfff19c16df.jpg[/img]
[h1]The economy![/h1]
Oh, the economy! Long ago something so important to us, but that has faded now. We wanted to keep offer and demand balanced, and give everyone equal opportunities, but this caused some restrictions on how obtainable everything was. Since we took the route of making Temtem as enjoyable and rewarding as possible, all those preventions were lifted, and new measures were introduced to make everyone happier.[b]Combat animations being skipped[/b]We finally added a setting many were asking for: the ability to skip battle animations!
For seasoned players who've already completed the campaign, for Luma and Umbra hunters, for competitive players looking to have matches that go straight into the point, there's a lot of different players who have been benefitting from this new setting that skips the Tems’ [b]entrance in battles,[/b] the [b]Technique animations [/b]and the [b]Status condition animations[/b]! This was an important step towards making Temtem more friendly and enjoyable, now that we weren't as worried about economic balance inside the game.
[b]Prices: down, rewards: up[/b]
[b]Prices have gone down[/b] aaaaall across the Archipelago. Teleporting has gone down, Luma Radars have gone down, all the purchasable items on the Breeding Center have gone down, some Tamer's Paradise tokens have gone down!
Some other prices have been adjusted to follow the trend set by players in the Trade House, like the prices of ETCs.
The net rewards of all NPC Tamers have been raised, and Dojo Parks were changed to reward [b]2 more Telomere Hotfixes[/b], and have a [b]better chance[/b] to drop a Telomere Hotfix: [b]Trait Swap[/b].
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/bdf00f0856cdf13e878c11258c5bdf9ae5e7ecd2.jpg[/img]
[h1]Away with restrictions: customize everything![/h1]
We opened the floodgates on [b]all cosmetics and Tamer Passes[/b], so the Archipelago has since become more colorful!
You can [b]select which Tamer Pass[/b] you want to work towards and complete it on your own terms and schedule. All Seasons and Passes are included, and you [b]can switch from one to another[/b] with total ease. Your progress is saved and kept every time you change, so you can go ahead without fear of missing progress or stepping back. You can only have one Tamer Pass active at one point, though!
But of course, we also completely [b]renovated the Store[/b] and changed the way it worked: for starters, it [b]no longer rotates[/b]! All previously Premium cosmetics are featured all the time, in an enormous, permanent recollection of cosmetics for you Tamers to purchase with Feathers. We added all the cosmetics previously obtained through Novas, and all items from past in-game events, the cosmetics from all Tamer Passes, and a handful of extra items that were currently outside of player’s reach, like common cosmetics and furniture.
The rotating nature of the Store was the main cause of FOMO that still remained in the game. Part of our desire for Temtem to be delightful for as long as possible without new updates included making sure there is no FOMO left, and offering a greater fan of customization possibilities for players.
We also added more cosmetics and housing furniture to the [b]Wishing Wells[/b] out of those available to purchase with Pansuns. Like the title says, customize everything!
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/d1b5d33cae2f1f2f44ce05b463c341e6e9614c67.jpg[/img]
[h1]A twirl to Ranked play[/h1]
As mentioned above, we also dedicated some time to adjusting and changing stuff around Competitive play, given its importance to us and the community.
The Season system was made obsolete with the changes to Tamer Passes and the Store. Since Seasons marked the time for rewards, balance changes, and a Tamer Matchmaking Rating (aka TMR) reset to keep the scene fresh and competitive, we needed to make sure Ranked PvP still had its Seasons. So, Ranked Seasons now automatically happen every two months, fitting perfectly inside our scheduled monthly maintenance. Every Season, TMR will get reset and adjusted based on current population, so the ranks make sense and challenge can still be sought. And you will get a [b]hefty sum of Feathers[/b] for your efforts!
We also [b]lowered the requirements to access PvP[/b], like the number of matches you need to play before you can queue for a ranked match, or how defining those matches are. We hope more people can easily hop into the competitive scene with resources like these and the Showdown feature, which allows you to create a team in 10 seconds.
If you are an endgame competitive players, you'll like to know that the minimum required Warriors for [b]Dojo Wars[/b] was reduced from [b]9 to 5[/b] members of a club, ensuring that clubs with less members can participate.
And for new and experimented players alike, we [b]made TMR easier to track[/b] and understand: from making it [b]visible[/b] at all times, to reducing its variance and making it as organic as possible, a request the community has held for a long time. With these changes, we hope more people can access and enjoy the Competitive side of Temtem, which we're really proud of and believe adds a lot of depth to the game!
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/1669136b51fb14ca8cc10832ae29c3e70b432be3.jpg[/img]
[h1]Crossing the finish line, and the road ahead[/h1]
This was Temtem’s last content-focused patch, marking the completion of a journey that began 8 years ago with our initial vision for the game.
When we first set out to create Temtem, we had a clear goal in mind: a world with six islands to explore and a roster of 141 Tems to tame. By the time we reached our desired finish line, we had expanded that vision far beyond its original scope, delivering 165 Tems, additional zones, and an experience that pushed the boundaries of what we had initially imagined.
Of course, growing beyond our original vision also brought along some challenges. Some were obstacles we overcame along the way, while others, like core system limitations, would require fundamental changes to the game’s structure. At this stage, making those changes would mean rewriting significant portions of Temtem rather than building upon it. Instead of focusing our efforts on reworking the foundations of Temtem, we’d rather use that time and the experience obtained to create something new, something that expands the universe of Temtem in fresh and exciting ways.
Temtem has grown into something truly special, thanks to the passion of our community and the dedication of our team. We’ve always wanted to make sure that every piece of content we created felt meaningful and polished, and we’re incredibly proud of what we’ve built together. Now, as we shift our focus to new adventures, we carry forward everything we’ve learned from Temtem to make our next project even more ambitious.
We’re grateful to everyone who has been part of this journey and can’t wait to share what’s coming next!
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/5c8a6d0855cc6391da2661724c09ca4c70657a71.jpg[/img]
[h1]Closing words[/h1]
This patch was huge, special and bittersweet: it was Temtem’s last big planned patch. Since then we've met back here a couple of times for bug fixes, corrections and the occasional balance changes, and these sort of irregular, need-based updates are still the plan for the future. We’ve reached our original goals for Temtem: fulfilled –even surpassed!– our planned roster of Tems and islands, and saw this game become bigger and greater than any of us could have ever dreamed of. As we kiss it complete, we wanted to put the cherry on top by making sure everyone, all of you who’ve been playing for years and any upcoming newbie snatching Temtem for the first time, can have the most enjoyable time while playing.
We hope you liked patch 1.8, and that it made Temtem nicer to play, replay and return to. We also hope you’ll stick with us as we discover new genres, learn and become better devs and continue enlarging the Temtem universe with more games, animated series, more merch and whatever the future brings, be it a future —yet still unplanned— [url=https://crema.gg/crema/a-word-on-temtem-and-cremas-future-an-open-letter-to-the-community/]Temtem 2[/url] or... this project Downbelow:
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/8bd0b6081df129c9e7f5bcbb8887f8381f7bb037.png[/img]
Have fun and Temtem up, Tamers!

patches.title: Temtem has reached 2 million users across all platforms!
patches.author: Tsukki
patches.content:
[b]Temtem has reached the 2 million mark on users!ːtemloveː
[/b]
How incredible is this? Thank you so much for making it possible, and for loving our game as much as we do!
[img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/2a13d30fe9b5d829cc705fc6a5ad7fb47e1004bf.jpg[/img]
Back when we were developing Temtem we wouldn't have believed you if you'd told us one day there'd be a million, let alone TWO millions of you. From the original Kickstarter Backers to the PSPlus friends we've made recentely, we're incredibly overjoyed that so many people have shared their time with our game, not to mention enjoyed it! Thank you, Tamers
[b]Here's to Temtem up, forever! 🥂[/b]

patches.title: The Oree Plushie has almost hatched - Over 70% funded!
patches.author: Tsukki
patches.content:
[h1]Hey Tamers![/h1]
Have we got a treat for you! Our digital friend Oree is this close to becoming a real-life huggable companion! The makeship campaign to bring Oree into the real world has soared past 70% of its funding: thanks for all your support so far!
[url=http://crm.gg/oreeplush][img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/d774c8bcdb22669f8accd3554b4b21cad484f30b.png[/img]
[/url]
This adorably big-headed little dude is absolutely bursting with terabytes of love and is still available for pre-order, but only for a limited time! We’re so close to reaching the goal and making sure that everyone who pre-orders gets to have their own little Oree at home.
Are you ready to help Oree find a home with you or your fellow tamers by pre-ordering it now? The Oree plushie [url=http://crm.gg/oreeplush]can be pre-ordered here![/url]
Get one for yourself, one for your mom, one for your waifu or husbando, or one for a friend, but do it before time runs out! 💖 We might’ve saved you a headache with all the holiday gifting going around these weeks!
The Oree plushie will be waiting for you in the snow, lighting one match after the other, thinking of how cozy your house must be… don’t let him down and you won’t regret it!
[h1]Temtem Up![/h1]

patches.title: Patch 1.8.2
patches.author: Tsukki
patches.content:
[h1]Important notice[/h1]
[list][*]The day for the Competitive Season change has been moved from 23/12 to [b]6/01[/b].
[/*][/list]
[h1]New Stuff[/h1]
[list] 	[*]A new pop-up message has been created for the Randomlocke mode, informing you that breaking co-op during a battle will cause you to lose that randomlocke run.[/*][/list]
[h1]Improvements[/h1]
[list] 	[*]We've made it so you can complete the Kudos and Weekly Challenges of completing a Radar if you capture the Luma at some point.[list] 	[*]They will still work if you complete the 300 encounters, but now we've added a completion condition for players who are unable to complete all 300 encounters because they find the Luma before.[/*][/list][/*] 	[*]We have modified Dojo Parks furnishing so there is always a straight path to the entrance.[list] 	[*]Dojo Parks that had furniture and decoration set in that path will have it returned to their inventory. You'll see them once you enter Edit mode.[/*][/list][/*] 	[*]We have adjusted the Triangle Head hat to have fixed dyes.[/*][/list]
[h1]Balance[/h1]
[h3]PvE[/h3]
[list] 	[*]The Techniques to Mythical Lair Bosses have been adjusted to better match their tamable versions.[/*][/list]
[h3]PvP[/h3]
[b]Temtem[/b][list] 	[*][b][b]#010[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Swali[/b][list] 	[*]Allergic Spread is now learned at level 27[/*][/list][/*] 	[*][b][b]#011[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Loali[/b][list] 	[*]Photosynthesis is now learned at level 35[/*] 	[*]Plague is now learned at level 49[/*] 	[*]Resin Trap is now learned at level 94[/*] 	[*][i]We wanted to equip Loali with better techniques in the early stages of the game, so Tamers can better enjoy the campaign with this Tem.[/i][/*][/list][/*] 	[*][b][b]#049[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Saku[/b][list] 	[*]Bark Shield has been added to its tech pool at level 73.[/*][/list][/*] 	[*][b][b]#050[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Valash[/b][list] 	[*]Undermine has been removed from its tech pool.[/*] 	[*]Double Edge has been added to its tech pool.[/*] 	[*][i]With the addition to Undermine in 1.8 and the buff to Madness Buff at the same time, we noticed that Valash was over-performing a little bit. However, we really liked the idea that Valash had access to a neutral special technique, so we decided to go with another, weaker option.[/i][/*][/list][/*] 	[*][b][b]#095[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Zizare[/b][list] 	[*]Narcoleptic Hit has been added to its tech pool at level 68.[/*] 	[*][i]Zizare was one of the latest monotype Temtem that had no access to any coverage technique. Narcoleptic Hit felt like a good fit and gave him an even more interesting niche, paired with his favorite partner Nagaise.[/i][/*][/list][/*][/list][list] 	[*][b][b]#108[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Goolder[/b][list] 	[*]Paralysing Poison has been added to its tech pool at level 96.[/*] 	[*][i]By adding Paralysing Poison to Goolder’s tech pool we wanted to give Goolder a more threatening option to punish players who tend to ignore him for too long.[/i][/*][/list][/*][/list][list] 	[*][b][b]#121[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Broccolem[/b][list] 	[*]Marathonist has been removed from its possible traits.[/*] 	[*]Strong Liver has been added to its possible traits.[/*] 	[*][i]This might be the biggest buff Broccolem has ever received. Since the removal of Strong Liver from Goolder on patch 1.6, this trait had disappeared from the competitive scene. This change is here to bring back the trait on a Temtem that we believe will make better use of it, allowing him to lose a weakness while not being immune to toxic ticks.[/i][/*][/list][/*][/list][list] 	[*][b][b]#160[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Monkko[/b][list] 	[*]Meditation has been removed from its possible traits.[/*] 	[*]Unnoticed has been added to its possible traits.[/*] 	[*][i]Because Meditation was a trait that saw near to no play, and instead of changing Monkko speed tiers, we decided to go with a high risk, high reward trait playstyle that we believe fits the Temtem perfectly.[/i][/*][/list][/*][/list][list] 	[*][b][b]#162[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Anatan[/b][list] 	[*]ATT has been increased from
50
=> 55.[/*] 	[*]SPATT has been decreased from
50
=> 45.[/*] 	[*]Cage has been added to his Technique Course learnset.[/*][/list][/*][/list][list] 	[*][b][b]#164[/b][/b] [b]—[/b] [b]Volgon[/b][list] 	[*]Inner Spirit has been removed from its tech pool.[/*] 	[*]Sparks has been added to its tech pool.[/*][/list][/*][/list] [b]Techniques[/b][list] 	[*]
[b]Cheer Up[/b]
[list] 	[*]
New effect added: It now also gives +1 SPATK to the target.
[/*][/list][/*] 	[*]
[b]Hallucination[/b]
[list] 	[*]
Exhaust turns have been reduced
2
=> 1
[/*][/list][/*][/list][list] 	[*]
[b]Hallucination+[/b]
[list] 	[*]
Priority has been reduced from
Very High
=> High
[/*] 	[*]
Base damage has been reduced from
100
=> 85
[/*] 	[*]
Exhaust turns have been reduced from
2
=> 1
[/*] 	[*]
[i]Hallucination+ became a little better than expected in 1.8.1. However, we really loved how it opened new sets and new strategies on some Temtem, which is why we decided to reduce its hitting power while keeping the same direction.[/i]
[/*][/list][/*] 	[*]
[b]Madness Buff[/b]
[list] 	[*]
SPATT buff has been reduced
+4
=> +3
[/*] 	[*]
[i]This change is here simply to reduce a bit the gain for special attackers that heavily rely on this technique to sweep, while aiding more defensive Temtem that might want to use this Technique, such as Magmut, for example.[/i]
[/*][/list][/*] 	[*]
[b]Trample[/b]
[list] 	[*]
Base damage has been increased
90
=> 110
[/*][/list][/*] 	[*]
[b]Trample+[/b]
[list] 	[*]
Base damage has been increased
90
=> 110
[/*] 	[*]
[i]Speaking of Magmut: with the nerf to the offensive part of Madness Buff, this change felt necessary to keep Magmut on track.[/i]
[/*][/list][/*][/list][b]Traits[/b][list] 	[*][b]Aerobic[/b][list] 	[*]Now only triggers when using an offensive wind technique.[/*][/list][/*] 	[*][b]Anaerobic[/b][list] 	[*]Now only triggers when using an offensive toxic technique.[/*][/list][/*] 	[*][b]Dispersion[/b][list] 	[*]Now only triggers when using damaging multi-target techniques.[/*][/list][/*] 	[*][b]Team Elusive[/b][list] 	[*]Now only prevents offensive multi-target techniques.[/*] 	[*][i]Note : This is a revert back to patch 1.4.2 changes. We hope to tone down a bit Vulffy’s usage and give some space to other Earth or Nature temtems.[/i][/*][/list][/*][/list]
[h1]Bug fixes[/h1]
[b]All Platforms[/b][list] 	[*]👥Fixed a hardlock while battling against Mimic Max in a co-op party.[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed being unable to change our turn 1 option if we have chosen to run away from a battle against a wild Temtem.[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed the Soulbound title requiring Kudos that were removed in patch 1.8, thus being impossible to 100% complete at the time.[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed not seeing the "captured" icon on wild Temtem if we have the Umbra version of it.[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed not being able to purchase the Tamer Pass using Feathers if we don't have enough Novas (Novas are no longer obtainable in Temtem).[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed not being able to complete a Radar Kudo after reaching 300 encounters.[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed a desynchronization in TemSafari bonuses after rerolling.[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed that the Lair bonus to deal more damage to enemy Tems affected by a Status Condition wasn't working properly.[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed not seeing Galios' faint animation if it had fainted through Doom after deactivating its trait with Inhibitor.[/*] 	[*]Fixed the indicator from triggering the Tireless trait not popping up.[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed receiving a "You're going to hand over this Temtem" message while using the Incubator Device if we had handed over a Temtem in that very game session.[/*] 	[*]👥Fixed seeing the inflated models of Umishi and Ukama while previewing a Seal.[/*] 	[*]Fixed some internal errors regarding Draft Arena NPCs.[/*][/list]
[i]Fixes, features, and improvements marked with the 👥 icon are originally suggested, inspired by, or reported by our community in our [url=http://discord.gg/Temtem]Discord server [/url]and [url=https://www.reddit.com/r/PlayTemtem/]subreddit[/url].[/i]

patches.title: Merch alert! Innki plushies are being created and ready for pre-order.
patches.author: Tsukki
patches.content:
Hey Tamers! Amidst all the chaos of [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/2510960/Temtem_Swarm/?utm_source=post&utm_campaign=demosnf&utm_medium=steam]Temtem: Swarm's free public Demo[/url], and [url=https://crema.gg/temtem/patch-1-8-1/]Temtem's 1.8.1 patch[/url], we still have one big surprise left: [b]merchandise!![/b]
One thing about Crema is that we don't have all that much merch out, so this is a golden opportunity to [b]grab a memento[/b] of your passage through the Archipelago!
I hereby present you, Innki:
[url=https://www.makeship.com/products/temtem-innki-plushie][img]{STEAM_CLAN_IMAGE}/32878068/3bc1ee77126e8dc6188f545ed18fec8ad0c2b0de.png[/img]
[/url]
[url=https://www.makeship.com/products/temtem-innki-plushie]Pre-orders are now available on the Makeship website![/url]
Get your hands on the [b]Innki Plushie[/b], the ultimate symbol of mastery for any Temtem tamer! Known as the most elusive Temtem in Cipanku, possessing an Innki is a testament to your skill and dedication. With soft, fluffy fur and bright, sparkling eyes, this elusive plushie is the perfect way to show off your status as a true Temtem expert. So, bring home your own Innki Plushie and let the world know you've reached the pinnacle of taming.
This is a Makeship campaign, so [b]we have a time limit[/b] to get this cute guy running! If you want to get a little scrimblo Innki to accompany you forever, make sure you pre-order it now!
Pre-orders are now available [url=https://www.makeship.com/products/temtem-innki-plushie]on the Makeship website![/url]
